figures_detected.description: Diagram showing cylinder bore grade numbers location on cylinder block and piston grade number location on piston crown, with FRONT orientation indicated.
Diagram showing connecting rod number location on connecting rod.
Illustration showing piston ring compressor tool being used to install piston with connecting rod into cylinder bore.
Photo showing installation of connecting rod bearing caps on engine.
Photo showing measurement of connecting rod side clearance.

steps.text: Install connecting rod bearings in connecting rods and connecting rod caps.
notes: Confirm that correct bearings are used. Refer to "Inspection".

warnings: Be careful not to scratch cylinder wall by connecting rod.
Arrange so that front mark on piston head faces toward front of engine.

sub_steps.text: Install them into corresponding cylinders with Tool.
Install connecting rod bearing caps. Tighten connecting rod bearing cap nuts to the specified torque.

torque_spec.step1: Tighten to 14 to 16 N·m (1.4 to 1.6 kg-m, 10 to 12 ft-lb).
torque_spec.step2: Turn nuts to 45° to 50° degrees clockwise with an angle wrench.

items.label: Connecting rod side clearance - Limit
items.condition: If beyond the limit, replace connecting rod and/or crankshaft.

content_blocks.text: If beyond the limit, replace connecting rod and/or crankshaft.

tools_referenced.name: Piston ring compressor
tools_referenced.usage: Used to compress piston rings when installing pistons with connecting rods into cylinders.

tools_referenced.name: Angle wrench
tools_referenced.usage: Used to tighten connecting rod bearing cap nuts an additional 45° to 50° clockwise.
